@@ -0,0 +1,49 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: e080
+project: keyboard-navigation
+title: Add enumerateVisibleScopes() API to SpatialFocusProvider
+---
+## What
+
+The Jump-To overlay needs to render a code label at the upper-left of every focusable scope visible right now. To do that it needs to enumerate all currently-registered scopes inside the topmost focus layer, with their fully-qualified moniker (FQM) AND their on-screen rect.
+
+Today `SpatialFocusProvider` (`kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx`) maintains an in-memory `Map<FullyQualifiedMoniker, FocusClaimListener>` for focus-claim notification, but it does NOT track the host element refs needed to read each scope's rect. Add that.
+
+Implementation:
+
+1. In `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx`:
+   - Extend the registration interface used by `<FocusScope>` (today `useFocusClaim(fq, listener)`) with an optional `hostRef: RefObject<HTMLElement | null>` parameter. Store the ref alongside the listener in the registry map: `Map<FullyQualifiedMoniker, { listener, hostRef, kind, layerName }>`.
+   - Capture `kind` (`"zone" | "scope"`) and the owning layer's name when the scope registers — both are available in `<FocusScope>` (kind is implicit; layerName comes from `FocusLayerZTierContext` or a dedicated `FocusLayerNameContext`).
+   - Expose a new actions method on `SpatialFocusActions`: `enumerateVisibleScopes(): Array<{ fq: FullyQualifiedMoniker, rect: DOMRect, kind: "zone" | "scope", layerName: LayerName }>`.
+   - Implementation: for each entry in the registry, if `entry.hostRef.current` is non-null AND its rect has non-zero area AND it is in the topmost layer (per the kernel's modal stack — query `spatial_top_layer` if such a Tauri command exists, otherwise read from a `FocusLayerStackContext`), include it; otherwise skip.
+   - Filtering by topmost layer is critical: when an inspector is open, the overlay should NOT enumerate scopes underneath in the board layer.
+
+2. In `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx`:
+   - Pass `hostRef` (the existing ref it already holds for `getBoundingClientRect`) through to the registration call.
+   - Same change in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-zone.tsx` (if `FocusZone` registers separately).
+
+3. Decide on rect provenance: read `hostRef.current.getBoundingClientRect()` at enumeration time — it's a one-shot read, the user is paused, no rect-staleness concern. Do NOT cache rects.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] `SpatialFocusActions` exposes `enumerateVisibleScopes()` returning `{ fq, rect, kind, layerName }[]`.
+- [ ] Returned list includes only scopes whose `hostRef.current` is non-null with non-zero rect AND lives in the topmost focus layer.
+- [ ] Returned rects come from a fresh `getBoundingClientRect()` call (no stale cache).
+- [ ] `<FocusScope>` and `<FocusZone>` register their host refs with the provider; no other call sites need to change.
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] New test `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.enumerate.test.tsx`:
+  - Mounts a tree with two `<FocusLayer>`s (a window layer and a modal/inspector layer), each containing several `<FocusScope>`s.
+  - Stubs `getBoundingClientRect` on each host (vitest jsdom convention; use the same helper the existing `*.spatial.test.tsx` files use).
+  - Calls `enumerateVisibleScopes()` and asserts the returned list contains only scopes inside the topmost (modal) layer, with the correct rects.
+  - Tests the empty case (no scopes registered) → returns `[]`.
+  - Tests the zero-rect case (scope registered but element is `display: none` or detached) → that scope is excluded.
+- [ ] Test command: `cd kanban-app/ui && pnpm test spatial-focus-context.enumerate` — passes.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd` — write the enumerate.test.tsx first; watch it fail (the actions method doesn't exist); add the API and update FocusScope; re-run.
\ No newline at end of file

## What

The Jump-To overlay needs to render a code label at the upper-left of every focusable scope visible right now. To do that it needs to enumerate all currently-registered scopes inside the topmost focus layer, with their fully-qualified moniker (FQM) AND their on-screen rect.

Today `SpatialFocusProvider` (`kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx`) maintains an in-memory `Map<FullyQualifiedMoniker, FocusClaimListener>` for focus-claim notification, but it does NOT track the host element refs needed to read each scope's rect. Add that.

Implementation:

1. In `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx`:
   - Extend the registration interface used by `<FocusScope>` (today `useFocusClaim(fq, listener)`) with an optional `hostRef: RefObject<HTMLElement | null>` parameter. Store the ref alongside the listener in the registry map: `Map<FullyQualifiedMoniker, { listener, hostRef, kind, layerName }>`.
   - Capture `kind` (`"zone" | "scope"`) and the owning layer's name when the scope registers — both are available in `<FocusScope>` (kind is implicit; layerName comes from `FocusLayerZTierContext` or a dedicated `FocusLayerNameContext`).
   - Expose a new actions method on `SpatialFocusActions`: `enumerateVisibleScopes(): Array<{ fq: FullyQualifiedMoniker, rect: DOMRect, kind: "zone" | "scope", layerName: LayerName }>`.
   - Implementation: for each entry in the registry, if `entry.hostRef.current` is non-null AND its rect has non-zero area AND it is in the topmost layer (per the kernel's modal stack — query `spatial_top_layer` if such a Tauri command exists, otherwise read from a `FocusLayerStackContext`), include it; otherwise skip.
   - Filtering by topmost layer is critical: when an inspector is open, the overlay should NOT enumerate scopes underneath in the board layer.

2. In `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx`:
   - Pass `hostRef` (the existing ref it already holds for `getBoundingClientRect`) through to the registration call.
   - Same change in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-zone.tsx` (if `FocusZone` registers separately).

3. Decide on rect provenance: read `hostRef.current.getBoundingClientRect()` at enumeration time — it's a one-shot read, the user is paused, no rect-staleness concern. Do NOT cache rects.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `SpatialFocusActions` exposes `enumerateVisibleScopes()` returning `{ fq, rect, kind, layerName }[]`.
- [ ] Returned list includes only scopes whose `hostRef.current` is non-null with non-zero rect AND lives in the topmost focus layer.
- [ ] Returned rects come from a fresh `getBoundingClientRect()` call (no stale cache).
- [ ] `<FocusScope>` and `<FocusZone>` register their host refs with the provider; no other call sites need to change.

## Tests

- [ ] New test `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.enumerate.test.tsx`:
  - Mounts a tree with two `<FocusLayer>`s (a window layer and a modal/inspector layer), each containing several `<FocusScope>`s.
  - Stubs `getBoundingClientRect` on each host (vitest jsdom convention; use the same helper the existing `*.spatial.test.tsx` files use).
  - Calls `enumerateVisibleScopes()` and asserts the returned list contains only scopes inside the topmost (modal) layer, with the correct rects.
  - Tests the empty case (no scopes registered) → returns `[]`.
  - Tests the zero-rect case (scope registered but element is `display: none` or detached) → that scope is excluded.
- [ ] Test command: `cd kanban-app/ui && pnpm test spatial-focus-context.enumerate` — passes.

## Workflow

- Use `/tdd` — write the enumerate.test.tsx first; watch it fail (the actions method doesn't exist); add the API and update FocusScope; re-run.

The registry exposes `entries()`, `has(fq)`, `size`, and `buildSnapshot(layerFq)`.
+Today `SpatialFocusProvider` (`kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx`) maintains an in-memory `Map<FullyQualifiedMoniker, FocusClaimListener>` for focus-claim notification, but it does NOT track host element refs (needed for rect lookup) or layer membership (needed for filtering). Add that.

-`SpatialFocusProvider` already maintains a `layerRegistriesRef: Map<FullyQualifiedMoniker, LayerScopeRegistry>` (populated via `actions.registerLayerRegistry`, used by the internal `buildSnapshotForFocused` helper).
-
-So this task is **NOT** "build a parallel registration map." It is: surface two new methods on `SpatialFocusActions` that read from the existing `layerRegistriesRef`.
-
-### Steps
+Implementation:

-1. In `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx`, add to the `SpatialFocusActions` interface:
-
-   ```ts
-   /**
-    * Enumerate every currently-registered scope in `layerFq`'s registry.
-    * Reads `getBoundingClientRect()` at call time for each entry's host
-    * ref. Returns `[]` when the layer has no registry, or when no entries
-    * have a live `ref.current`.
-    */
-   enumerateScopesInLayer: (
-     layerFq: FullyQualifiedMoniker,
-   ) => Array<{ fq: FullyQualifiedMoniker; rect: DOMRect }>;
+1. In `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx`:
+   - Extend the registration interface used by `<FocusScope>` (today `useFocusClaim(fq, listener)`) with two new optional parameters: `hostRef: RefObject<HTMLElement | null>` and `layerFq: FullyQualifiedMoniker` (the FQM of the enclosing `<FocusLayer>`). Store all four pieces in the registry map: `Map<FullyQualifiedMoniker, { listener, hostRef, kind, layerFq }>`.
+   - The layer FQM is already available to descendants via `LayerFqContext` (see `kanban-app/ui/src/components/layer-fq-context.tsx` referenced from `focus-layer.tsx:68`). `<FocusScope>` can read it with `useContext(LayerFqContext)` and pass it to the registration call.
+   - Expose a new actions method on `SpatialFocusActions`: `enumerateScopesInLayer(layerFq: FullyQualifiedMoniker): Array<{ fq: FullyQualifiedMoniker, rect: DOMRect, kind: "zone" | "scope" }>`. Takes the layer FQM the caller wants to enumerate; does NOT auto-pick "topmost". The caller (Jump-To overlay) picks the layer it's covering.
+   - Implementation: for each entry in the registry where `entry.layerFq === layerFq`, if `entry.hostRef.current` is non-null AND its rect has non-zero area, include it with a fresh `getBoundingClientRect()` call; otherwise skip.
+   - Also expose a helper: `actions.layerFqOf(fq: FullyQualifiedMoniker): FullyQualifiedMoniker | null` — returns the layer FQM a given scope's registration is in. The Jump-To overlay needs this to derive "the layer the user was focused in before the overlay opened" from the prior focused FQM.

-   /**
-    * Look up the layer FQM whose `LayerScopeRegistry` currently contains
-    * `fq`. Returns `null` when no registry has the FQM (transient unmount
-    * window, or unregistered FQM).
-    */
-   layerFqOf: (fq: FullyQualifiedMoniker) => FullyQualifiedMoniker | null;
-   ```
+2. In `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx`:
+   - Pass `hostRef` (the existing ref it already holds for `getBoundingClientRect`) AND `layerFq` (read from `LayerFqContext`) through to the registration call.
+   - Same change in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-zone.tsx` (FocusZone registers separately — verify by reading the file).

-2. Implementation in the provider:
-
-   - `enumerateScopesInLayer(layerFq)`: look up `layerRegistriesRef.current.get(layerFq)`; if `undefined`, return `[]`. Otherwise walk `registry.entries()`, skip entries whose `ref.current` is null, and return `{ fq, rect: ref.current.getBoundingClientRect() }`. (No need to surface `kind` — only `<FocusScope>` registers in the registry; `<FocusZone>` does not, so every entry is a scope by construction.)
-   - `layerFqOf(fq)`: walk `layerRegistriesRef.current.entries()`, return the first `layerFq` whose `registry.has(fq)` is true; return `null` if none match. Same pattern as the internal `buildSnapshotForFocused` helper.
-
-3. Wire both into the `actions` bag returned by the provider so the JumpToOverlay (next task) can call them through `useSpatialFocusActions()`.
-
-4. **Do NOT modify** `<FocusScope>`, `<FocusZone>`, `LayerScopeRegistry`, or `ScopeEntry`. The existing registration shape is sufficient. The earlier version of this task proposed extending the per-FQM claim map with `hostRef` / `layerFq` parameters — that's now redundant because `LayerScopeRegistry` already holds those.
-
-### Why no `kind` field
-
-The earlier version of this task included `kind: "zone" | "scope"` in the return shape. Drop it: `LayerScopeRegistry` only tracks `<FocusScope>` registrations (verified at `focus-scope.tsx` `useOptionalLayerScopeRegistry()` call site; `focus-zone.tsx` does not call it). Zones are containers, not Jump-To targets. Every enumerated entry is a scope.
+3. Rect provenance: read `hostRef.current.getBoundingClientRect()` at enumeration time — one-shot read, the user is paused, no stale-rect concern. Do NOT cache rects.
